mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity valuations higher over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signals broad-based demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward 60-day returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 103 indicates supply-side overheating and capacity constraints, which trigger input cost inflation and margin compression, depressing equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or INDPRO peaks precede market rallies rather than declines.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, providing fundamental support that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month window.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns, or earnings revisions turn negative despite positive GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.0 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive during sustained CPI spikes above 2.0%, or the signal fails to predict market declines when inflation accelerates faster than expected.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 200,000 combined with an unemployment rate below 6.0 percent confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative or flat forward 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or PAYEMS surprises fail to move equity prices when unemployment is low.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 650,000 units signals sustained residential investment and wealth effects, which transmit to broader equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day equity returns are negative when HOUST crosses above 650, or housing construction data decouples from broader economic growth and equity performance.
